extraction.one_line_summary: GODOT Bjorg won from a breakaway that survived by 18 seconds inside final 3km after peloton miscalculated the chase.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo chased all day despite no GC threat in break, potentially exhausting domestiques before final mountain stage.
Breakaway survival with 18-second gap at 3km collapsing to zero at line indicates sprinter teams misjudged final terrain resistance.
LAPORTE led chase despite being Jumbo's sprint option, suggesting team prioritized controlling gaps over optimizing leadout positioning.

rider_signals.GODOT Bjorg: positive: First pro win at 23 suggests emerging breakaway specialist, especially on reduced-bunch stages with late categorized climbs.
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: neutral: Doing chase work instead of conserving for sprint reveals Jumbo's internal sprint hierarchy ambiguity.
rider_signals.MADIOT Valentin: positive: Dominated KOM points throughout stage, indicating aggressive breakaway selection strategy targeting secondary competitions.
